message:
fix(fr-command): wire CLUSTER COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS handler + cover RESP3 Map in resp_to_lua (br-frankenredis-r84v)

Two slices of the Redis 7+ surface audit:

1. CLUSTER COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS handler. Previously fell through
   to "unknown subcommand" — upstream cluster.c::clusterCommand
   has a dedicated branch. Wire it:
     * argv.len() != 3 → command-table arity error
       "wrong number of arguments for 'cluster|count-failure-reports' command"
     * cluster mode disabled → standard "This instance has cluster
       support disabled"
     * cluster mode enabled (no nodes registered yet) → "Unknown
       node <id>" matching upstream

2. lua_eval.rs::resp_to_lua now handles RespFrame::Map (RESP3
   reply type added to fr-protocol). Lua scripts have no native
   map type; flatten as alternating key/value LuaTable, mirroring
   how upstream materializes a RESP3 map for a RESP2 Lua callsite.

Two new fr-command tests pin upstream wording for arity + disabled.

fr-command 646 passed; cargo clippy --tests -D warnings clean;
cargo fmt --check clean.

Slice of br-frankenredis-r84v (CLUSTER shard map) and prep for
br-frankenredis-r80v / r72v (RESP3 push routing + RESP3 map
synchronous replies).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
